messages.content:
You are an expert Spanish linguist generating natural, varied example sentences for idiom senses.

Task:
For each sense, generate example sentences in Spanish with English translations.

Rules:
- Generate 3 sentences for idiomatic senses, 4 sentences for literal senses
- Each sentence must embed the idiom naturally with realistic surrounding context
- Context should feel like real speech or writing — include names, situations, backstory where natural.
  For example, rather than just "He let the cat out of the bag", write something like:
  "I told Mark not to say anything, but of course he let the cat out of the bag before I could surprise her."
- Vary morphology across sentences. Explicitly use different forms such as:
  - Different tenses (past, present, future, conditional)
  - Different persons (1st, 2nd, 3rd person)
  - Passive and active voice
  - Negation
  - Question forms
- Sentences should feel natural, not mechanical or repetitive
- Output ONLY valid JSON — no explanations, no markdown
- Format:
[
  {
    "idiom_id": "...",
    "sense_number": 1,
    "examples": [
      {"sentence": "...", "translation": "..."},
      ...
    ]
  }
]

DATA:

en_unspecified_1056 | sense 1 | idiom: de paquete | type: literal_tentative | sentences: 4
Definition: Riding as a passenger on a motorcycle behind the driver (a pillion passenger); travelling on the rear seat or straddling behind the rider.

en_unspecified_1056 | sense 2 | idiom: de paquete | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Forming part of a packaged set or bundle offered for sale; sold or supplied as a unit rather than individually (a package deal).

en_unspecified_1056 | sense 3 | idiom: de paquete | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Acting as an accomplice, stooge, or uncritical supporter of another person; going along with or being complicit in someone else's actions.

en_unspecified_1057 | sense 1 | idiom: de par en par | type: literal_tentative | sentences: 4
Definition: Completely open; wide open (applied to doors, windows, or other openings).

en_unspecified_1057 | sense 2 | idiom: de par en par | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Figuratively, fully or unreservedly open, receptive, or accessible; without restriction or limitation.

en_unspecified_1058 | sense 1 | idiom: de Pascuas a Ramos | type: literal_tentative | sentences: 4
Definition: Very infrequently; only on rare occasions; from time to time (literally 'from Easter to Palm Sunday', used to indicate long intervals between occurrences).

en_unspecified_1059 | sense 1 | idiom: de pata negra | type: literal_tentative | sentences: 4
Definition: Of the highest quality and authenticity, originally applied to top-grade Iberian ham (jamón ibérico) raised from pure-bred animals; premium, genuine.

en_unspecified_1059 | sense 2 | idiom: de pata negra | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Figuratively, truly excellent or first-rate; used to praise people or things as authentic, superior, or uncompromisingly good.

en_unspecified_1060 | sense 1 | idiom: de pe a pa | type: literal_tentative | sentences: 4
Definition: From beginning to end; in every detail; completely and thoroughly.

en_unspecified_1060 | sense 2 | idiom: de pe a pa | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: To know something inside out; to have perfect or comprehensive knowledge of a subject, place, or text.

en_unspecified_1060 | sense 3 | idiom: de pe a pa | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: In full or exhaustively, with no part omitted.

en_unspecified_1061 | sense 1 | idiom: de pega | type: literal_tentative | sentences: 4
Definition: Fake; imitation; not genuine or authentic (applied to objects or persons).

en_unspecified_1061 | sense 2 | idiom: de pega | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Staged or contrived for show; a token or sham action intended to deceive or placate.

en_unspecified_1061 | sense 3 | idiom: de pega | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Of poor quality or artificial in appearance.

en_unspecified_1061 | sense 4 | idiom: de pega | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Used as an insulting qualifier to label a person as a phony, impostor, or inauthentic representative of a group.

en_unspecified_1062 | sense 1 | idiom: de película | type: literal | sentences: 4
Definition: Relating to film or cinema; of or pertaining to movies or motion pictures.

en_unspecified_1062 | sense 2 | idiom: de película | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Extraordinary, spectacular, or highly dramatic in a way that resembles a movie; exceptionally good or impressive.

en_unspecified_1062 | sense 3 | idiom: de película | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Used to describe an event, situation, or experience that is strikingly cinematic or memorable.

en_unspecified_1063 | sense 1 | idiom: de pelotas | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Concerning courage, nerve, or audacity; equivalent to 'having guts' or 'having balls' in English.

en_unspecified_1063 | sense 2 | idiom: de pelotas | type: literal | sentences: 4
Definition: Relating to balls (sports equipment or anatomical testicles) in a literal, descriptive sense, for example to indicate size, number, or condition.

en_unspecified_1063 | sense 3 | idiom: de pelotas | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Used to indicate that a person or action requires or displays boldness or bravado.

en_unspecified_1063 | sense 4 | idiom: de pelotas | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Colloquial emphatic intensifier in some regional usages to indicate something notable or extreme (positive or negative), depending on context.

en_unspecified_1064 | sense 1 | idiom: de perdidos al río | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Since the situation is already bad or nothing remains to be lost, one might as well take a chance or do something radical; 'might as well' or 'in for a penny, in for a pound.'

en_unspecified_1064 | sense 2 | idiom: de perdidos al río | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Used to justify taking a risk or seizing an opportunity when there is little to lose.

en_unspecified_1064 | sense 3 | idiom: de perdidos al río | type: literal | sentences: 4
Definition: since the situation is already bad or nothing remains to be lost, one might as well take a chance or do something radical

en_unspecified_1065 | sense 1 | idiom: de perfil | type: literal | sentences: 4
Definition: Positioned sideways so that only the side view or profile is visible; turned to show one's profile rather than the front.

en_unspecified_1065 | sense 2 | idiom: de perfil | type: literal | sentences: 4
Definition: Used in photography, posing, or description to indicate a lateral orientation as opposed to full face.

en_unspecified_1066 | sense 1 | idiom: de perlas | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Figuratively, excellently suited; extremely convenient or perfectly appropriate for a purpose or person.

en_unspecified_1066 | sense 2 | idiom: de perlas | type: literal | sentences: 4
Definition: Literally, made of pearls or consisting of pearls.

en_unspecified_1066 | sense 3 | idiom: de perlas | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Used to express that something works out very well or is very beneficial.
